title: Human Urine on Wool
timeEstimate: Pro-lane within hours; cold blot only at home
stainChemistry: Human urine is less concentrated than pet urine but it still has the same crystal-forming residue that grips fibers. On wool, the standard urine fix (retail enzyme cleaner) is BANNED — those broad-spectrum enzymes would damage the wool itself. Hot water sets the urine residue AND felts wool at the same time. Alkaline cleaners (OxiClean) felt wool and lock urine in. Wool's narrow tolerance window is cold water plus dilute acid plus the minimum amount of moisture plus zero agitation. Pro uses a wool-safe targeted enzyme plus cold acid plus cold extraction. Home protocol is gloves plus cold blot plus pro within hours.
whyThisWorks: Cold press-blot extracts bulk urine before crystals lock in. A wool specialist can then use a wool-safe targeted enzyme plus cold mild acid plus cold rinse to clear what's left without felting. Home cannot match this: every retail urine product contains broad-spectrum enzymes that damage wool, and hot rinse cycles felt the fiber.
scienceNote: Mechanism (pro-facing): human urine is urea + uric acid crystals + salts + protein; uric acid reactivates with humidity. Wool is keratin protein. Pro uses urease-only enzyme (NOT protease — protease digests wool keratin) plus cold acetic acid plus cold extraction.
defaultAssumption: Wool + human urine = pro within hours. Mattresses with urine on wool = pro early because over-wetting is impossible. Garments easier than mattresses but still pro-lane.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Gloves on (biohazard). Optional UV blacklight in dark to find full extent — urine spreads farther than visible.

Press cloth dampened with COLD water on the BACK of the spot (if garment) or directly on wool surface. Vertical pressure only — no rubbing. Cold dilutes uric acid + prevents protein setting + reduces felt risk.
Cold NSD sponge press. Suspends fresh urea + protein without enzymes.
Pro applies urease-ONLY enzyme (digests uric acid; protease would digest wool). Strict application without flooding.
Diluted acetic acid neutralizes residual ammonia. STRICT 60-sec ceiling. Flush cold immediately.
Cold final rinse. Press between dry towels, lay flat to air-dry (or for wool carpet — weighted overnight + fan).

steps: 1. Gloves. Cold press-blot at first contact.
3. Wool-safe urease-ONLY enzyme (NOT protease — digests wool).
5. Cold final rinse, press flat to air-dry.
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold throughout — wool felts above 30°C when wet
warnings: NEVER protease enzymes — digest wool keratin
NEVER hot water / steam — felts wool + sets protein
NEVER alkaline (OxiClean / ammonia / baking soda) — felts wool + locks urine
NEVER agitation — felts wool on contact
NEVER pour solution on wool — flood = felt

homeSolutions.instruction: Human urine on wool is pro-lane because every standard urine fix damages wool. Retail enzyme cleaners contain protease which DIGESTS WOOL. Hot water rinses felt wool. Alkaline cleaners (OxiClean) felt wool AND lock urine. Home protocol = gloves + cold press-blot, pro within hours.
Disposable gloves — biohazard. Especially if not your urine.

Turn garment inside out (if applicable). Press a clean white cloth dampened with COLD water onto the BACK of the spot. Vertical pressure only — never rub or scrub. Cold dilutes the uric acid + prevents protein setting. Lift, refresh cloth, press. Never pour water on wool — flood + agitation = felt.
Press the wool between two dry white towels — stack, press, lift moisture. No wringing, no twisting. Lay flat at room temperature.

homeSolutions.agent: Pro Wool Specialist — What to Tell Them
homeSolutions.instruction: Don't apply retail enzyme cleaners — they digest wool. Tell the pro: "Human urine on wool, age [hours / overnight], gloves + cold press-blot ONLY at home. NO enzyme cleaner, NO hot water, NO OxiClean, NO baking soda, NO household products applied. Need wool-safe urease-only enzyme + cold rinse."

steps: 2. Gloves on. NO retail enzyme cleaners — they digest wool.
3. Cold damp-cloth press-blot — vertical pressure, no rubbing.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop after cold press-blot. Any retail enzyme cleaner on wool digests the wool.
warnings: NEVER Nature's Miracle / any retail urine enzyme — DIGESTS WOOL
NEVER hot water — felts wool + sets protein
NEVER OxiClean / alkaline / baking soda — felts wool + locks urine

neverDo: Never retail urine enzyme cleaners on wool — digests wool
Never baking soda on wool urine
fiberSensitivities: Wool keratin felts when wet + warm + agitated
Urease-only enzymes are wool-safe but pro-only
Aluminum-protein complex (from sweat-mixed urine) sets faster on wool

materialWarnings: Pro within hours — retail enzymes destroy wool
Cold press-blot is the safe home action
Wool-safe pet-urine chemistry exists but is pro-only
Aluminum + heat sets urine on wool permanently

escalation.when: Always — wool + urine is pro-lane within hours. Earlier for valuable garments, mattress topping, or set-in / dried urine.
escalation.whatToTell: "Human urine on wool, age [hours / overnight], gloves + cold press-blot ONLY at home. NO enzyme cleaner, NO hot water, NO OxiClean, NO baking soda applied. Need wool-safe urease-only enzyme + cold rinse."
escalation.specialistType: professional dry cleaner specializing in wool (call ahead — confirm urease-only enzyme + cold-water-only protocol)

contextQuestions: Garment / wool blanket / wool carpet (use pet-urine-wool-carpet for pet on wool carpet)?
Has anyone applied retail enzyme cleaner / OxiClean / baking soda?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Mattress with urine through to wool topping = different escalation (low pro threshold, can't be flooded). Pre-applied retail enzyme = wool likely partially digested where applied; disclose. Pre-applied OxiClean = wool likely partially felted + urine locked; disclose.
